perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall be required to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall aim to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of 'shall be required to purchase' to 'shall aim to purchase' introduces a contradiction. Initially, Premier had a mandatory purchasing obligation. Now, it only has a goal or target. This change contradicts the section's initial purpose of establishing a firm commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Section 2(c) in its entirety and replace it with the following: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall aim to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019. For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will be required to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Premier 'shall be required to purchase' a MAOV, creating a firm obligation. The changed text replaces this with 'shall aim to purchase', which introduces a goal rather than a requirement. The Contradicted Text reinforces this goal-oriented language. This creates an in-text contradiction as a 'requirement' is directly contradicted by an 'aim', creating uncertainty regarding Premier's purchasing obligations under the contract.

perturbation.original_text: For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will be required to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.changed_text: For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will attempt to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be required to purchase' to 'will attempt to purchase' creates an in-text contradiction. Previously, Premier had a clear obligation. Now, the text suggests a less forceful intention. This conflicts with the mandatory nature of the MAOV initially intended for the entire term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Section 2(c) in its entirety and replace it with the following: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall aim to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019. For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will attempt to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'Premier will be required to purchase [***]' creating a definite obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'Premier will attempt to purchase [***]' indicating a less forceful obligation. This contradicts the intention of a Minimum Annual Order Volume (MAOV) throughout the term of the agreement, as 'attempt' does not guarantee purchase, thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition: 'Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'
